articles.title: Micron Price Prediction: The Forecast Flags a Big Pullback
articles.snippet: Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU | MU Price Prediction) has gone vertical.

articles.title: Baird Strategist Warns Memory Chip Boom Won't Last. Margins Will Compress as Hyperscalers Seek Alternatives
articles.snippet: Baird Investment Strategist Ross Mayfield recently appeared on CNBC to push back against the prevailing narrative that the memory cycle has been permanently rewired by AI demand, framing the bull case for Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU | MU Price Prediction) as a position-management problem rather than a definite outcome.

articles.title: $900 Billion Just Vanished as Korean Chip Stocks Crater, but the Squawk Crew Isn't Sold on an AI Breakdown
articles.snippet: Four straight down sessions have erased more than $900 billion in equity value, with the proximate cause tracing to Seoul.

articles.title: Micron Earnings Take on New Gravity With Market on Edge Over AI
articles.snippet: Tuesday's stock-market slide was what one Wall Street strategist called a “chip-wreck.” The semiconductor companies that have led this year's stock-market advance with triple-digit percentage gains were suddenly hit with a wave of doubts about the sustainability of the AI-driven rally.

articles.title: Micron And 4 AI Stocks For Data Center Growth
articles.snippet: Global data center capacity could nearly double by 2030, driven by surging AI demand and rapid cloud expansion. Goldman Sachs expects hyperscaler AI spending to surpass $1 trillion in 2027 as workloads require more compute, memory, storage, and connectivity. Micron's earnings could serve as an indicator of data center growth, with some of the largest beneficiaries found inside the rack, including providers of chips, storage, and networking solutions.

articles.title: Micron earnings are set to send the market on a wild ride — and a new ETF may add to the volatility
articles.snippet: The Roundhill T-REX 2X Long DRAM Daily Target ETF (RAM) is a 2x levered version of the popular DRAM ETF.

articles.title: What to Watch for in Micron Earnings
articles.snippet: Micron, which will publish earnings after the bell, has become one of the most important stocks in the world. Shares are up nearly 300% this year.

articles.title: The NASDAQ Dropped 3% as Micron, Intel, and AMD Tumble, but One Tech Name Is Bucking the Trend
articles.snippet: CNBC's Dominic Chu opened Tuesday's market coverage with a familiar tape pattern that has not shown up much in 2026. "The tech sell-off that began Monday building momentum this morning after memory chip related shares tanked on Asian markets overnight," Chu told viewers, and damage in U.S. premarket concentrated exactly where expected. articles.snippet: CJ Muse went on CNBC this morning and made the case that the memory cycle most investors are watching does not actually peak where they think it peaks.

articles.title: Stay "Cautious" as Tech Concentration Builds in SPX Ahead of MU Earnings
articles.snippet: @CharlesSchwab's Nathan Peterson turns to the S&P 500 (SPX) price action and explains why he's "cautious" as concentrated positioning ramps up in the index. He tells investors to brace for Micron's (MU) earnings as the stock seeks to jump a high bar.

articles.title: Alphabet replacing Verizon in Dow Jones Industrial Average index
articles.snippet: Alphabet Inc (NASDAQ:GOOG) will join the Dow Jones Industrial Average, replacing Verizon Communications Inc (NYSE:VZ, XETRA:BAC), in a reshuffle that further increases the index's exposure to large-cap technology companies. S&P Dow Jones Indices said the change will take effect prior to the opening of trading on June 29, 2026.

articles.title: Adobe Is Priced for Too Much Bad News
articles.snippet: Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) has had a brutal year, and the ADBE stock forecast has rarely been more contested. Shares have fallen from above $400 in mid-2025 to around $202 today — a roughly 50% decline — driven by a CFO departure, investor skepticism about AI monetization timelines, and broad multiple compression across software.

articles.title: Novo Nordisk: A Strong Yield And Continued Growth
articles.snippet: Novo Nordisk A/S remains a valuable investment, boasting strong FCF, a >4% dividend yield, and continued R&D commitment despite waning initial Ozempic excitement. NVO maintains robust operating margins (46.9% in Q1 2026) and expects $6.4 billion FCF in 2026, supporting both capex ramp-up and shareholder returns. The pipeline is anchored by GLP-1 and obesity drugs, with new oral semaglutide formats and recent Akero acquisition targeting multi-billion dollar future sales.

articles.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Microsoft Corporation Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
articles.snippet: New class action for Microsoft (MSFT) urges investors to seek recovery for alleged securities fraud violations – lead plaintiff deadline of 8/11/2026

articles.title: DEADLINE ALERT for ERAS, NNOX, MSFT: Law Offices of Howard G. Smith Reminds Investors of Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Class Actions
articles.snippet: BENSALEM, Pa., June 24, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Law Offices of Howard G. Smith reminds investors that class action lawsuits have been filed on behalf of shareholders of the following publicly-traded companies. Investors have until the deadlines listed below to file a lead plaintiff motion.

articles.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Microsoft Corporation Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
articles.snippet: NEW YORK, June 24, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Bronstein, Gewirtz and Grossman, LLC, a nationally recognized investor-rights law firm, announces that a class action lawsuit has been filed against Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ: MSFT) and certain of its officers. This lawsuit seeks to recover damages against Defendants for alleged violations of the federal securities laws on behalf of all persons and entities that purchased or otherwise acquired Microsoft securities between May 1, 2025 and January 28, 2026, both dates inclusive (the "Class Period").

articles.title: Critique challenges Microsoft's quantum computing claims
articles.snippet: A critique from the University of St Andrews published in the journal Nature provides evidence that Microsoft's claimed quantum computing "breakthrough" was built on flawed foundations.
